mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of months where UNRATE crosses above the 5.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 200.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses the 200.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining federal funds rate signals accommodative policy supporting labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth indicates immediate consumer spending strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of low market volatility.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when market volatility indices exceed historical 75th percentile thresholds.
